dashboard.label: Getting Started
dashboard.description: First-run setup & configuration guide for MCP-Watch.

html.div: Welcome to MCP-Watch 👋 MCP-Watch reads only Splunk's built-in _audit / _internal data — there is nothing to ingest. To start seeing data you just need to tell it which account(s) your MCP server uses. Follow the steps below; the panels on this page show your current configuration and whether data is flowing.

panel.title: Step 1 — Who is your MCP agent? (top non-internal users, 24h)

search.query: `audit_index` action=search info=granted NOT [ | inputlookup mcp_excluded_users | fields user ] | stats count as searches, latest(_time) as last by user | sort - searches | head 10 | convert ctime(last) | rename last as last_search | appendpipe [stats count | where count=0 | eval user="(no real / MCP user has run a query yet)", searches="—", last_search="head to Step 2 →"] | fields - count

html.div:
Step 2 — Add your MCP user(s) to the lookup. Two ways:
Splunk UI: Settings → Lookups → Lookup table files → edit mcp_users.csv (needs the Lookup Editor, or edit on disk).
SPL (no extra app): run once in Search —
| inputlookup mcp_users.csv | where user!="mcp_service_account" | append [| makeresults | eval user="YOUR_MCP_USER", role="mcp_agent", description="my MCP server" | table user role description] | table user role description | outputlookup mcp_users.csv
(where user!="mcp_service_account" drops the default placeholder row at the same time.)
Step 3 — Custom / non-official MCP server? If your MCP doesn't use the official Splunk MCP Server, add its client user-agent pattern to mcp_user_agents.csv so the MCP Detection (REST) dashboard catches it.
Default exclusions. Splunk's reserved identities (admin, splunk-system-user, sidecar_agent-manager, nobody) are filtered out of every discovery panel via mcp_excluded_users.csv — they are never real MCP agents (any user with the admin role inherits every capability, and the official server's own housekeeping calls run as splunk-system-user with the same user-agent as real traffic). If your deployment really does use admin as the MCP service account, edit the lookup and remove that row.
Step 4 — Distributed / Search Head Cluster? Deploy this app via your Deployer ($SPLUNK_HOME/etc/shcluster/apps/ → splunk apply shcluster-bundle), not directly on each search head.
Splunk Cloud with remapped indexes? Override the audit_index / internal_index macros in local/macros.conf.
Then wait ~5 minutes (scheduled searches) and open MCP Overview. Full docs: see the app's README.md.

single.option: events — 0 means no watched user is configured / active
